purpose: Spawns and manages Claude Code CLI worker processes for swarm tasks. Builds the args list (--print, --output-format stream-json, --verbose, --add-dir for Synapse + project dirs, --append-system-prompt, --dangerously-skip-permissions or --permission-prompt-tool stdio + --input-format stream-json), feeds the prompt via stdin, parses NDJSON output line-by-line and rebroadcasts each parsed event to the renderer over `worker-output`. Tracks every spawned process in `activeWorkers` keyed by PID for kill/dispatch/inspect. Forwards stderr verbatim as a separate worker-output frame so the UI can display tool activity. Emits `worker-complete` with full output + exit code on close, and `worker-error` on spawn failures.
gotchas: ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE and CLAUDECODE env vars MUST be deleted before spawn — otherwise the child Claude binary detects nested Claude/Electron context and either misbehaves or refuses to run.

Output is NDJSON. The line-buffer split-on-newline strategy means the FINAL chunk often does not end with `\n`; the close handler explicitly flushes `worker.lineBuffer` or the terminal `result` event would be lost (this bug was already fixed and the comment highlights the exact failure mode).

When dangerouslySkipPermissions=false the service switches to `--permission-prompt-tool stdio` + `--input-format stream-json`, wraps the prompt in an NDJSON `{type:'user',message:{role:'user',content:promptText}}` envelope, and KEEPS stdin open so the renderer can relay permission decisions via writeToWorker(). Forgetting either flag breaks the permission relay.

`--add-dir` is added FIRST for synapseRoot then for projectDir — workers must always have access to Synapse/agent/instructions or the worker protocol cannot be loaded. Order matters because the agent reads instructions before project files.
Any worker resolved-dir that equals synapseRoot or projectDir is deduped — passing duplicates would cause the CLI to error.
`SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID` env var is injected so PreToolUse hooks (.claude/hooks/) can enforce dashboard isolation. Removing this breaks the validate-master-write/validate-progress-file hooks.
There is a stale `buildArgs(opts)` function declared AFTER module.exports — it is dead code (older path that did NOT inject synapse_root or context dirs). Do not refactor callers to use it.

The 10s `gotOutput` watchdog references `worker.errorOutput` from a closure but `worker` is declared AFTER that setTimeout — the closure works because of var-hoisting only; refactoring to const/let would break it.
patterns: PID-keyed activeWorkers map is the single source of truth; every callback (stdout/stderr/close/error) checks-and-mutates this map.
All renderer notifications are pushed over three named channels: `worker-output`, `worker-complete`, `worker-error` — kept stable so ipc-handlers/preload only needs to expose those three.
stream-json NDJSON parsing: each line is JSON.parse'd inside a try/catch and forwarded as `{chunk, parsed}` so the renderer can use either the raw text or structured event.
killWorker double-tap: SIGTERM, then a 5s SIGKILL fallback to guarantee dead workers.
conventions: Console-log every lifecycle transition prefixed with `[ClaudeCodeService]` for easy log-grepping.
All public functions take a single `opts` object (taskId, dashboardId, projectDir, prompt, systemPrompt, model, cliPath, dangerouslySkipPermissions, additionalContextDirs, resumeSessionId).
Return shape from spawnWorker is intentionally minimal `{pid, taskId, dashboardId}` — full state lives in the activeWorkers map and is fetched via getActiveWorkers().
